opus_correction: Deep sentry analysis identifying 5 critical findings: GBP_JPY churning (6 trades in 3.4h, cooldown gate bypassed due to NULL finding_id), finding_id regression from snipe_direct refactor, S16 SAR flip 0-for-4 losing 89.2 pips, RSI as strongest loss predictor (winners 69.9 vs losers 41.2), indicator coverage regression at 47.2%
opus_reasoning: The sentry detected that finding_id NULL on 100% of trades breaks cooldown enforcement because the gate queries by finding_id. The snipe_direct path bypasses the watch-finding-trade chain entirely. RSI divergence between winners and losers suggests gating SELL entries when RSI < 40.

task: Root cause analysis of serve_ui crashes and DB corruption
opus_correction: Three-layer root cause analysis: (1) SSE client dict not thread-safe — concurrent iteration+mutation kills threads, fix with threading.Lock on all access points; (2) db_pool stale -shm detection — flock deadlocks on FUSE mounts, new approach uses SELECT 1 health check on fresh connections as proof of stale -shm; (3) Dashboard polling amplifier — 6 simultaneous 30s intervals creating 10-13 concurrent DB hits, staggered to 5s offsets reducing max concurrent to 2
opus_reasoning: The crash cascade has three layers: the trigger (SSE RuntimeError), the sustained damage (stale -shm preventing DB recovery), and the amplifier (simultaneous polling hammering the broken pool). Each fix addresses one layer. The key insight for db_pool is that a brand-new connection failing SELECT 1 with disk I/O error is proof the -shm is stale — no live process could corrupt between connect() and SELECT 1.
